new_classes.label_en: Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) wine region
new_classes.definition_en: A geographic site that is officially recognised as a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) for wine under EU law (Chapter 22, Additional Note 6(a)), within which qualifying wines must be produced to bear the PDO designation.

new_classes.label_en: PDO wine production process (CN 2204 21, containers ≤ 2 l)
new_classes.label_de: Herstellungsprozess für Wein mit g.U. (KN 2204 21, Behältnisse ≤ 2 l)
new_classes.definition_en: Production process of wine from fresh grapes that occurs within a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) region as defined by Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN, yielding wine for presentation in containers of 2 litres or less.

new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.), anderer, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (KN 2204 21 68)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as referred to in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, other than the specific PDO wines enumerated in CN codes 2204 21 11 to 2204 21 64, presented in containers of 2 litres or less.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 68: (1) wine with a PDO (modelled via PDOWineRegionSite + PDOWineProductionProcess + producedBy chain); (2) presentation in containers of ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer restriction); (3) 'other' PDO wines not enumerated in codes 2204 21 11–2204 21 64 (complement on the known named PDO sibling, Tokaj 2204 21 23, is included). However, full coverage is limited because: (a) the complete list of specifically named PDO wines covered by codes 2204 21 11 through 2204 21 64 (approximately 11 named appellations) has not yet been modelled in this TBox session, so complement restrictions to all those sibling classes cannot be expressed — only the Tokaj sibling already present in the running TBox is excluded explicitly; (b) Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 contains a detailed enumeration of named PDO appellations that would need to be represented as individual PDO site subclasses to fully discriminate 2204 21 68 from its siblings. The PDO quality itself and the 'other' residual nature are partially captured through structure, but completeness is constrained by the scope of the running TBox.
